avoid_months: July and August (monsoon, not listed as open)
snow_or_rain_notes: Summer: snow from Lower Waterfall campsite in May; from Upper Waterfall in June; Dhanderas Thatch to Ronti Gad mostly under snow until last week of May; upper stretches (Rata Pheri to Ronti Gad) still under snow in second half of June
Autumn: snow mostly missing; frost at Upper Waterfall campsite; chances of snowfall at Rupin Pass when weather goes bad; first snows of the season hit the pass around middle of October
Monsoon season (July-August) is not listed as an open season; autumn begins as monsoons fade
temperature_notes: Summer: daytime 13°C to 17°C; night drops to zero at higher campsites; 4 warm layers required
Autumn: daytime 12°C to 16°C; night drops to zero to -4 to -5°C at higher campsites; 4 warm layers required

stated_difficulty: Difficult trek in terms of terrain and climbs to well over 15,000 ft
experience_suitability: Fitness helps in making your trek easier
terrain_challenges: Frequent river crossings along the Rupin River
Massive snow bridges in May and June
Makeshift log bridges set up by shepherds in September and October
Hopping on boulders or using ropes if no bridges exist
altitude_or_weather_risks: Trek ascends to 15,279 ft above sea level
At altitudes above 10,000 ft, chances of Acute Mountain Sickness are real
Altitude sickness can happen to anybody including first-time and experienced trekkers
Even a fit person climbing too high too soon runs equal risk of developing AMS
Indiahikes itinerary allows enough space for acclimatising; no more than 2000 ft elevation gain per day

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog or run 5 km in under 32 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it you will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the fitness criteria
Record runs using apps like Nike or Strava and paste your public profile link on your Indiahikes account
Fitness record may be rejected if you fail to link your fitness profile or do not meet the required fitness level at least three times in the past month
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
6-week plan available for those not currently active

experience_themes: Snow bridges (including the massive Rupin Waterfall snow bridge)
Snowfields and snow slides from Dhanderas Thatch to Ronti Gad
White moonscape-like snowfields at Rata Pheri in May
Combination of greenery and snow in first half of June
Resplendent green meadows with wildflowers at Dhanderas Thatch in early June
Hundreds of waterfalls along the trail in autumn
primary_watchouts: First snows around middle of October make trekking dangerous and difficult; Indiahikes winds up autumn season by mid-October
Snow bridge and snow sections require caution in summer
Night temperatures drop to zero or below at higher campsites in both seasons
Trekkers planning beyond mid-October (autumn season ends)
Trekkers seeking guaranteed snow in autumn (snow mostly missing)
Frequent river crossings along the Rupin River
best_for: Trekkers seeking a classic snow experience (summer)
Trekkers wanting a combination of greenery and snow (first half of June)
Trekkers seeking waterfalls, wildflower meadows, and golden valley views (autumn)
not_ideal_for: Trekkers planning beyond mid-October (autumn season ends)
Trekkers seeking guaranteed snow in autumn (snow mostly missing)
First-time trekkers (implied by 'Suitable for Experienced Trekkers' and AMS warning that 'Altitude sickness can happen to anybody - first-time trekkers as well as experienced trekkers')
